member.office_address: 2332 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3213

form_analysis.form_instructions_text: Please use this form to submit Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27) appropriations program or language requests to Rep. Adriano Espaillat's office. If you have multiple requests, please submit a separate form for each request. Please provide as much of the requested information as possible to ensure we can fully evaluate your request. **DO NOT USE THIS FORM FOR COMMUNITY PROJECT FUNDING REQUESTS** All program and language requests must be submitted through this form in order to be considered. Please don't hesitate to reach out to our office with any questions. You may contact my team at Nia.Thomas@mail.house.gov and Joseph.Newman@mail.house.gov

fields.label: E-mail address
Appropriations Bill / Subcommittee of Jurisdiction
options: AG: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
CJS: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
EW: Energy and Water Development, and Related Agencies
FSGG: Financial Services and General Government
INT: Interior, Environment, and Related Agencies
LHHS: Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
MILCON: Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
NSRP: National Security, Department of State, and Related Agencies
THUD: Transportation, and Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: CJS: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded through the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies (CJS) Appropriations subcommittee.

fields.label: Federal Agency or Sub-Agency (See here for an index of agencies: https://www.usa.gov/federal-agencies)
fields.rationale: Standardized name for the agency overseeing the Science Mission Directorate as per reference values.

fields.label: Federal Account - See here for an index of accounts: https://espaillat.house.gov/sites/evo-subsites/espaillat.house.gov/files/evo-media-document/espaillat-agency-accounts-1.pdf
fields.rationale: The account name 'Science' is the standard appropriations account for the Science Mission Directorate within the NASA budget.

fields.label: Please select whether this is a Program (funding) or Language request.
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this as a programmatic funding request for $9 billion.

fields.label: Funding choice
options: Support the enacted funding level of $ (see below)
Support President Trump's FY2026 Budget request.
fields.rationale: This option allows us to specify the specific funding ask of $9 billion, which is an increase over the FY2026 enacted level and essential for restoring stability to the SMD workforce and research programs in NY-13 at institutions like Columbia University.

fields.label: FY27 Request Amount (required if choice made above says "see below")
fields.rationale: This matches the authoritative funding ask for NASA's Science Mission Directorate provided in the reference values.

fields.label: FY27 President's Budget Requested Amount (if applicable)
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not been released yet, according to the provided SMD funding history.

fields.label: FY26 Enacted Amount (if applicable)
fields.rationale: This is the pre-filled enacted amount for FY2026 as stated in the reference values and H.R. 6938.

fields.label: Program Description: Please provide a brief description of the program and justification for federal support at the funding level you are requesting:
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to restore funding levels and advance peer-reviewed decadal survey priorities
* Restores agency stability and workforce capacity following the significant funding disruptions of FY 2026
* Ensures U.S. leadership in space exploration and technology against increasing international competition
* Supports a high-tech industrial base and academic research pipeline across all 50 states, including $75.2M in NY-13 since FY2023
fields.rationale: This response uses the specific $9B ask, references the FY26 context, and incorporates the local spending total of $75.2M from the fact sheet to justify federal support.

fields.label: Additional Information: If you would like to include more specific information about this request's impact on communities in New York's 13th Congressional District, please do so here:
fields.draft_value:
* $75,207,386 in total NASA Science obligations within NY-13 between FY2023 and FY2025
* Significant local research activity at Columbia University, including a $10.8M grant for Earth climate change and planetary habitability modeling
* Major technology development contracts for local firms Scispace LLC ($17.6M) and Trinnovim LLC ($17M)
* Supports over 200 individual grants and contracts to district institutions, fostering a robust local STEM workforce
fields.rationale: Cites exact total spending for NY-13 and lists specific top grant and contract recipients (Columbia University, Scispace, Trinnovim) with their associated dollar amounts from the fact sheet.

fields.label: Is a Congress Member leading a letter on this issue? If Yes, please note who and include the appropriate contact information.
fields.draft_value: Yes. Representatives Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading the House programmatic support letter for NASA Science.
fields.rationale: The reference values confirm an existing House letter led by Reps. Bacon and Chu.

fields.label: Text of proposed language:
fields.rationale: The request is programmatic and the context specifies N/A for bill or report language; this section appears to be for language requests.

fields.label: Please provide a brief description of and justification for your request.
fields.draft_value:
* $9 billion programmatic request for NASA's Science Mission Directorate to sustain mission timelines and scientific output
* Critical for maintaining the U.S. competitive edge in space science, particularly in response to China's expanding lunar and planetary ambitions
* Provides vital federal support for scientific roadmaps that drive national innovation and workforce stability
fields.rationale: Provides a concise version of the programmatic justification based on the $9B ask and advocacy talking points regarding international competition.

fields.label: OPTIONAL: Please feel free to provide any additional information you think will assist in our evaluation of your request, including impact to communities in New York's 13th Congressional District or national implications.
fields.draft_value:
* Space science delivers a 3-to-1 economic return on investment, benefiting the broader aerospace and technology economy
* Direct district benefits through five active grants at Columbia University totaling over $32M for research including climate change, dark matter, cosmic mapping, and space sample data archiving
* The United States leads the world in space science because of sustained, bipartisan investment over decades; this leadership is neither automatic nor easily recoverable once lost
fields.rationale: Combines the national economic argument with specific local data on active grants at Columbia University from the fact sheet.

fields.label: Is a Congress Member leading a letter on this issue? If Yes, please note who and include the appropriate contact information.
fields.draft_value: * Yes, Reps. Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading a House programmatic support letter for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.
fields.rationale: Identifies the current House leads for the SMD support letter as provided in the reference values.

issues.field_label: OPTIONAL: Please feel free to provide any additional information you think will assist in our evaluation of your request, including impact to communities in New York's 13th Congressional District or national implications.
issues.issue: The draft value of over $32M for five active grants at Columbia University is a cumulative multi-year award total ($32,114,780), not an annual spending figure.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: 23-nup2023-0090  interdisciplinary research on Earth and planetary system modeling  the impacts of climate change  and the habitability of other worlds 2023-2026
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Modeling Earth climate change and planetary habitability research.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: The gaps experiment: a search for dark matter using low-energy antiparticles
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Searching for dark matter using low-energy antiparticles.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced balloon instrumentation for cosmic background mapping.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: The department of applied mathematics and physics (APAM) at Columbia University (CU) proposes to renew cooperative agreement nnx15aj05a with the NASA goddard institute for space studies. The scope of work spans three broad themes. Earth system modeling will involve the development and evaluation of atmospheric  oceanic  land surface and cryospheric model components to better understand past natural and anthropogenic changes in climate and  as a central part of the us contribution to internationallycoordinated efforts to make projections of possible future climates. Research in comparative planetary climatologies will extend the Earth system modeling effort to better understand the conditions under which other planets in our solar system have been habitable  and how we might detect habitable planets at greater distances. Remote sensing research will include fundamental work in radiative transfer  and significant involvement in major field campaigns to better measure aerosols  clouds  and ocean surface properties. The cooperative agreement is the administrative mechanism through which the unique expertise of NASA and apam can be leveraged to advance our understanding of the Earth system  other planets  and cutting edge measurements  building upon the rich scientific history of both institutions.
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing advanced climate models for Earth and other planets.

top_grants.recipient_name: The Trustees of Columbia University in the City of New York
top_grants.description: The astromaterials data system: enabling fair astromaterials samples data through trusted archiving services and broad community engagement
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Building a trusted data archive for NASA's space samples.
